description_long: This object is an elongated, teardrop-shaped stone with a pointed tip and a broad, roughly triangular base. Its surface is uneven and rugged, featuring visible chips, ridges, and natural indentations, suggesting it may have been shaped or weathered over time. The coloration is a consistent earthy brown, with subtle variations in tone and some darker patches. One side of the stone displays faint markings or inscriptions, possibly catalog numbers or identifiers. The stone's edges are sharp and angular, especially near the tip, while the base is thicker and more irregular. Its overall appearance is primitive and utilitarian, reminiscent of a hand tool or naturally occurring stone used as an obstacle or barrier in an urban setting.
description: A rugged, brown, teardrop-shaped stone with a pointed tip, rough surface, and visible markings on one side. Its angular edges and irregular base give it a primitive, utilitarian look.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full teardrop shape, broad base, and pointed tip, highlighting the stone's rough texture.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the stone's thin profile and sharp edges, with visible surface ridges and some markings.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the irregular base and more pronounced surface indentations, along with visible catalog markings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizes the elongated, narrow profile and angular contours of the stone.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular, natural mottling and chipping with no artificial carvings; some faint catalog markings on one side.
attribute_weathering: Rough and chipped, with visible signs of natural weathering and minor surface abrasions.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or manicured urban surfaces, but could blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements.
